# Transcrição: Understanding_Meta_Event_Parameters

## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Lumer. Let's go! Let's talk about event parameters. Yeah, so you can track events on your website, so both standard and custom events, using the Metapixel and Convergence API. Now those events represent things like purchases, registrations, searches, leads, and a whole lot more. Of course, we haven't gotten into the custom events yet at this point. By themselves, these events can help with reporting, optimization, and targeting. So you can now know how many people who clicked your ad purchased, or you can optimize for people who have purchased before, or you can target people who have purchased. But you should also be sending event parameters to send more details about these actions. So examples of parameters for purchase would be currency, value, content name, basically the product name, content ID, or product ID, number of items purchased. So you can then create, let's say, custom audiences to target people based on those parameters. For example, you can then create a website custom audience, not just based on the general purchase event, but you can refine by a parameter to isolate purchases by whatever the accompanying parameters are. So for example, target people based on the value of your purchase, the currency used, the name of the product, so just those people who bought a certain product, the category of the product. You can then also refine by aggregated value. So create an audience of people who made a certain number of purchases, or based on the total amount spent, or average amount spent, or minimum amount spent, there's all kinds of audiences you can create there. I also do this with custom events for quality traffic activity. So I send parameters like the scroll depth, time on page, the audio player has been played, an embedded video, YouTube video has been played, and I can then create an audience for one of those events and isolate the audience by parameter. So I can segment the specific YouTube video that people watched on my website using parameters, or segment the people who played my audio player at least five times, or segment the people who viewed at least five pages of my website for at least two minutes. It's the parameters that give you granularity and power. So you can use this power for more granular reporting, using the custom conversions, and super granular targeting, which is my favorite. So if you need more help creating standard events with parameters,
